name: @chbrain/khai-engine-group
description: khai engine content, the group domain: how two or more people develop a shared pattern over time in a repeated context.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Group development as process: how two or more people develop a shared pattern over time -- forming, storming, norming, performing, adjourning -- in a context that repeats.

card.wire: Attaches at Process. The anchor (process_evolve_group.md) declares the full arc a group runs as it develops over a repeated context, and the position (position_group.md) names what a group is -- a standing field configuration. A group has a current stage, not a permanent one.
card.issue: Five stage processes a group may be running right now -- forming, storming, norming, performing, adjourning -- plus the position that names the group itself. The same group is in storming this morning and norming this afternoon, and may regress to forming before the week ends; the room reads the stage from the texture of the exchange.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor, so the group's stage is read wherever personas meet in a repeated context. The link: a group instance links the engine's group position for its universal shape, and the scene reads the stage the moment is in.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and a real stage or the group position is linked. Whether the stage is true to what is happening between the personas is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Name the group as a position; read the current stage from scene context, not a label. The stage shows in the texture of the exchange -- polite reconnaissance, surfaced friction, settled rule, executing pattern, or dissolving mass.
